# Sprint 1 Backlog — RAG Refinement System

**Sprint:** Sprint 1 (2-week iteration)
**Date:** 2026-06-06
**Theme:** Foundation thin-slice + mandated data-handling compliance substrate.

---

## 1. Sprint Goal

> **Stand up a deployable, authenticated, tenant-isolated ingestion-and-routing thin slice: a user can upload a PDF, have its native TOC extracted and section-aware chunks embedded into the dual store, and have a query routed to the relevant sections — running on CI-gated, health-checked services with DPDP x-pii data-access in place from day one.**

This proves the core "scope before search" mechanism end-to-end (upload -> TOC -> chunk -> embed -> store -> route) on production-shaped infrastructure, and lands the data-handling compliance substrate (tenant isolation, secrets/SAST gate, x-pii access) before any PII is processed at scale.

---

## 2. Team Capacity Assumption

| Parameter | Value | Basis |
|-----------|------:|-------|
| Team size | 5 engineers | DB, DevOps, Data, AI, Backend (per AGREED CONTRACTS roster) |
| Sprint length | 2 weeks (10 working days) | Standard iteration |
| Per-engineer velocity (greenfield, Sprint 1) | ~8.4 SP | Conservative first-sprint velocity; no historical baseline |
| **Gross capacity** | **42 SP** | 5 x 8.4 (greenfield ramp already folded into the per-engineer figure) |
| Commitment ceiling (95%) | **39.9 SP** | Hard cap: committed SP <= capacity x 0.95 |
| **Committed SP** | **39 SP** | 92.9% utilization — under the 39.9 ceiling |

> Velocity is an estimate, not a measurement (no prior sprint exists). Sprint 2 will recalibrate capacity from Sprint 1 actuals. The 95% ceiling leaves slack for the unknowns inherent in a first greenfield sprint.

---

---

## 4. Why these (foundation-first rationale)

- **Schema & ops first.** STORY-001 (Postgres L1/L2 + tenant_id), STORY-002 (Qdrant payload), STORY-004 (Docker + health/ready), STORY-005 (CI + SAST/secrets gate) are the substrate every later story builds on. CI and health checks make every subsequent story testable and deployable (AGREED CONTRACT devops).
- **Auth/tenant substrate early.** STORY-038 lands API-key + OAuth2/JWT and — critically — the `tenant_id` scoping path that targeted retrieval (STORY-017, Sprint 2) and IDOR guards (STORY-026) depend on. Sequenced second so schema (tenant_id column) precedes it.
- **Ingestion thin slice.** STORY-008 (upload), STORY-009 (native TOC), STORY-011 (section-aware chunking), STORY-003 (embed + upsert) complete the ingest path for Scenario-A documents — the highest-coverage, lowest-risk TOC scenario. Pseudo-TOC (STORY-010, Scenario B/C) and OCR (STORY-012) defer to Sprint 2.
- **Router lands in Sprint 1.** STORY-014 is the product's core differentiator and a P0-risk item; getting it into Sprint 1 (with golden-TOC fixtures and the RouterOutput-contract validation/fallback per ADV-004) de-risks the whole thesis early. The targeted-retrieval consumer (STORY-017) and confidence-gated fallback (STORY-015) follow in Sprint 2.
- **Mandated data-handling compliance.** Because Sprint 1 ingests and stores document PII, the data-handling compliance substrate is pulled in per the auto-create rule: STORY-005 (secrets/SAST security gate), STORY-038 (audit-logging + rate-limit substrate, session-fixation review hook), and **STORY-034 (DPDP access + `x-pii` inventory, FR-029)**. The CERT-In 6-hour incident-reporting hook (STORY-037, full 5 SP) and DPDP erasure (STORY-033), no-retention (STORY-035), and residency (STORY-036) are scheduled for Sprint 2 — their security-scanning and audit-logging substrate begins in Sprint 1 via STORY-005 + STORY-038 DoD, so no PII is processed without a security gate and audit trail.

---

## 5. Sprint-1 Definition of Done (sprint-level)

The global DoD (backlog.md Section 1.3) applies to every story. Sprint-1 is "Done" when:

1. All 11 stories meet the global DoD; CI green (lint+type+unit+SAST+secrets).
2. End-to-end thin-slice demo: upload a bookmarked PDF -> native TOC extracted -> section-aware chunks embedded into Qdrant + sections in Postgres -> a query routes to the correct section(s) with `routing_time_ms` in [100,300].
3. Services deploy via Docker; `/health` and `/ready` green; `/ready` returns 503 when a dependency is down.
4. Tenant isolation enforced: every row + every Qdrant payload carries `tenant_id`; no cross-tenant read path exists.
5. DPDP: `GET /v1/documents/{id}/data` returns a `DataAccessExport` with `pii_fields[]`; `x-pii` annotation honored (FR-029).
6. P0-risk stories (STORY-011 chunking, STORY-014 router) carry deterministic golden fixtures.
7. No secrets in code/repo (CI secrets scan green); secrets via 12-factor env only.
8. `fr_story_traceability.json` updated; sprint review against this goal recorded.

---

## 6. Out of Sprint 1 (next-sprint candidates)

Pseudo-TOC (STORY-010), OCR (STORY-012), multilingual (STORY-013), targeted retrieval (STORY-017), fallback gate (STORY-015), query rewrite (STORY-016), generation+SSE (STORY-018), citations (STORY-019), confidence/explainability data (STORY-020/021), `/v1/route` (STORY-024), all frontend (STORY-028..032), DPDP erasure/no-retention/residency (STORY-033/035/036), CERT-In hook (STORY-037), hybrid/rerank (STORY-022/023), SDKs/dashboard/feedback/fine-tune (STORY-007/027/032/...). See `backlog.md` for the full WSJF-ordered set.

---

*Sprint 1 | 11 stories | 39 SP committed vs 39.9 SP ceiling (42 SP capacity) | 92.9% utilization | foundation-first + mandated data-handling compliance substrate.*
